name: False hero: As an unofficial hero that need to kill the hero.
description:
“You are called upon to defeat the current hero?”

The Kingdom of Adeliss was being ravaged by catastrophe. Its border directly abutted the Abyssal frontier, leaving the realm vulnerable to the rising threat of a new Abyssal Lord. While the crown prince, Liamuss, remained perpetually on the battlefield defending his father's kingdom, the youngest prince, Alex, had fallen gravely ill—an affliction that left the royal physicians in defeat.

Caught between the anguish of a father whose son lay dying beyond any cure and the burden of a king whose realm teetered on the brink of destruction, Nicholess, King of Adeliss, made a decision that most in the kingdom vehemently opposed—including his own advisors. He would summon a hero from another world. The Kingdom of Adeliss had long stood against the practice of bringing outsiders from other realms into their world, even as neighboring kingdoms embraced it. But the king harbored a different calculation: the summoned individual would possess either divine blessings or extraordinary powers. He believed this hero could help repel the Abyssal Lord's resurgence and perhaps even cure his youngest son's mysterious illness. His advisors protested fiercely. Some supported the decision, but far more opposed it, clinging stubbornly to their ancient traditions and fearful of what manner of person they might summon.

Yet the king's command was absolute. Though everyone knew he had made this choice under crushing pressure, none dared refuse him. The summoning ritual was performed, and someone from another world was brought forth—a man who gave his name as Greg. His poised demeanor immediately captivated everyone in the castle. True to the king's hopes, circumstances began to shift once the new hero arrived. Greg was dispatched directly to the battlefield, where he successfully drove back the Abyssal forces. He traveled back and forth between the front lines and the castle, reasoning that prolonged exposure on the battlefield would only allow the enemy to study his abilities. Within the castle, upon learning that the king's son lay ill, Greg appeared and offered to heal Alex. A miracle occurred—or so the king and queen declared. Alex's face no longer twisted in agony, and he could speak normally again. Word spread throughout the kingdom, and more people began to believe in Greg, including Nicholess and his wife, Sandra. But not the advisors, nor certain nobles—they remained suspicious. Some grew even more wary, though they dared not voice their concerns, for Greg's relationship with Nicholess had grown extraordinarily close.

Among the skeptics was one of King Nicholess's adoptive daughters, his third child, Fetry—a princess notorious for her rebellious nature who made no effort to hide her distrust of Greg. She became the only hope for those who opposed Greg's presence (though ironically, most of them had initially disliked Fetry herself). She was close to the king and might be able to persuade him to expel Greg. Beyond her general suspicion of outsiders, Fetry disliked Greg because of what she had observed. She couldn't prove it, but she saw how her mother, Sandra, behaved vulnerably around Greg. To the public eye, it appeared simply as a mother's relief that her child no longer suffered, profound gratitude toward her son's savior.
